item.title: Any way to specify default -D options?
item.author: ralsina
item.content:encoded: I have some libraries that work differently depending on what -D flags you pass. In some programs that use them, only some combinations of -D flags work.I am currently doing this via makefiles and similar things.Would it make sense to add something like that to shard.yml somehow so “shards build” does the right thing?

item.title: Mixed memory model for Crystal
item.author: ComputerMage
item.content:encoded: What do you think about the mixed memory model, where the compiler decides for itself what allocation to use for the variable: stack, slab ARC, atomic ARC, or, as a last resort, it will be GC?Will it strike your interest in the Crystal even more? May this feature give Crystal a competitive advantage, and will Go and Rust be sunset?Really love to hear your suggestions and cool ideas!

item.title: Favorite underrated Shard?
item.author: Kritoke
item.content:encoded: What is an underrated shard you use in almost every project that more people should know about?

item.title: Crystal 2026 Predictions
item.author: matthewmcgarvey
item.content:encoded: The time has come to peer into the crystal ball and tell me your predictions for 2026. Do you think Crystal is going to pivot into a functional language? You think a major startup will adopt Crystal? Is this the year of the Crystal LSP? Or maybe you’ve got a project in the works that will come out this year. Here’s your chance to let us all know what you think.

item.title: Announcing CrImage - Pure Crystal Image Processing Library
item.author: naqvis
item.content:encoded:
I’m excited to share , a comprehensive image processing library written entirely in Crystal with zero external dependencies.A complete image toolkit that handles everything from basic format conversion to advanced effects, text rendering, and even QR code generation — all in pure Crystal. PNG, JPEG, GIF (animated!), BMP, TIFF, WebP, ICO Lines, circles, polygons, Bézier curves, gradients, anti-aliased rendering, chart helpers Resize (nearest/bilinear/bicubic/lanczos), rotate, crop, flip Blur, sharpen, edge detection, sepia, vignette, temperature adjustment Built-in TrueType/OpenType/WOFF font engine with kerning and ligatures — no FreeType needed QR codes, blurhash, smart crop, watermarks, thumbnails, EXIF reading, image comparison (SSIM/PSNR) Decompression bomb protection built-inrequire "crimage"
img = CrImage.read("photo.jpg") .resize(800, 600, method: :lanczos) .brightness(10) .sepia
CrImage::PNG.write("vintage.png", img)
# Generate a QR code qr = CrImage.qr_code("https://crystal-lang.org") CrImage::PNG.write("qr.png", qr)
17 posts - 7 participants
